truth.papers.A81.title: Strong yet ductile low-density steel via multiphase heterogeneous microstructure
truth.papers.A81.paper_scope: Development of a low-density steel with a multi-phase heterogeneous microstructure to achieve high strength and ductility, and analysis of strengthening and stress concentration mitigation mechanisms.

prediction.papers.A81.title: Strong yet ductile low-density steel via multiphase heterogeneous microstructure
prediction.papers.A81.paper_scope: Development of low-density Fe-C-Mn-Al-Si steel with multiphase heterogeneous microstructure via practical thermomechanical processing; investigation of strengthening mechanisms, TRIP effect, back stress, and stress-buffering behavior through experiments and constitutive modeling.

prediction.alloys.alloy_fe025c3mn2al04si.alloys_not: Eliminates costly Nb, Mo, and Ni; slight increase in Mn and Al content for lightweight structural applications. Density reduction achieved via Al addition.

truth.processes.proc_fe025c3mn2al04si_750.desc: Hot rolled, cold rolled, intercritical annealed at 750 °C for 30 min, air cooled.
truth.processes.proc_fe025c3mn2al04si_750.proc: Prior hot rolling: heating at 1200 °C for 60 min, hot rolling from 1100 °C with six passes to 90% reduction, finish at 880 °C, air cooling. Cold rolling: 60% reduction to 1.6 mm.
truth.processes.proc_fe025c3mn2al04si_800.desc: Hot rolled, cold rolled, intercritical annealed at 800 °C for 30 min, air cooled.
truth.processes.proc_fe025c3mn2al04si_800.proc: Prior hot rolling: heating at 1200 °C for 60 min, hot rolling from 1100 °C with six passes to 90% reduction, finish at 880 °C, air cooling. Cold rolling: 60% reduction to 1.6 mm.
truth.processes.proc_fe025c3mn2al04si_850.desc: Hot rolled, cold rolled, intercritical annealed at 850 °C for 30 min, air cooled.
truth.processes.proc_fe025c3mn2al04si_850.proc: Prior hot rolling: heating at 1200 °C for 60 min, hot rolling from 1100 °C with six passes to 90% reduction, finish at 880 °C, air cooling. Cold rolling: 60% reduction to 1.6 mm.
truth.processes.proc_fe025c3mn2al04si_750T.des: Hot rolled, cold rolled, intercritical annealed at 750 °C for 30 min, air cooled, then low bainite aging at 400 °C for 30 min.
truth.processes.proc_fe025c3mn2al04si_750T.pro: Prior hot rolling: heating at 1200 °C for 60 min, hot rolling from 1100 °C with six passes to 90% reduction, finish at 880 °C, air cooling. Cold rolling: 60% reduction to 1.6 mm.
truth.processes.proc_fe025c3mn2al04si_800T.des: Hot rolled, cold rolled, intercritical annealed at 800 °C for 30 min, air cooled, then low bainite aging at 400 °C for 30 min.
truth.processes.proc_fe025c3mn2al04si_800T.pro: Prior hot rolling: heating at 1200 °C for 60 min, hot rolling from 1100 °C with six passes to 90% reduction, finish at 880 °C, air cooling. Cold rolling: 60% reduction to 1.6 mm.
truth.processes.proc_fe025c3mn2al04si_850T.des: Hot rolled, cold rolled, intercritical annealed at 850 °C for 30 min, air cooled, then low bainite aging at 400 °C for 30 min.
truth.processes.proc_fe025c3mn2al04si_850T.pro: Prior hot rolling: heating at 1200 °C for 60 min, hot rolling from 1100 °C with six passes to 90% reduction, finish at 880 °C, air cooling. Cold rolling: 60% reduction to 1.6 mm.

prediction.processes.proc_fe025c3mn2al04si_750T.des: Vacuum induction melting, hot forging, hot rolling with 90% reduction (1100–880°C), air cooling, cold rolling 60%, intercritical annealing at 750°C for 30 min with air cooling, and low bainite aging at 400°C for 30 min.
prediction.processes.proc_fe025c3mn2al04si_750T.pro: Critical temperatures: Ac1=705°C, Ac3=950°C, Ms=346°C, Bf=380°C.
prediction.processes.proc_fe025c3mn2al04si_800T.des: Vacuum induction melting, hot forging, hot rolling with 90% reduction (1100–880°C), air cooling, cold rolling 60%, intercritical annealing at 800°C for 30 min with air cooling, and low bainite aging at 400°C for 30 min.
prediction.processes.proc_fe025c3mn2al04si_800T.pro: Critical temperatures: Ac1=705°C, Ac3=950°C, Ms=346°C, Bf=380°C.
prediction.processes.proc_fe025c3mn2al04si_850T.des: Vacuum induction melting, hot forging, hot rolling with 90% reduction (1100–880°C), air cooling, cold rolling 60%, intercritical annealing at 850°C for 30 min with air cooling, and low bainite aging at 400°C for 30 min.
prediction.processes.proc_fe025c3mn2al04si_850T.pro: Critical temperatures: Ac1=705°C, Ac3=950°C, Ms=346°C, Bf=380°C.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_750T.processing_: Aging in lower bainite region; designated AN750-T.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_800T.processing_: Aging in lower bainite region; designated AN800-T.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_850T.processing_: Aging in lower bainite region; designated AN850-T.

truth.structures.sample_750T.overall_structure: Multiphase microstructure consisting of ferrite, bainite/tempered martensite, fresh martensite, and retained austenite.
truth.structures.sample_750T.microstructure_li: Initial ρ0 (10^14 m^{-2}): F 0.33, B/TM 0.44, FM 0.86, RA 0.81. GNDs pile up at phase boundaries.
In B: carbides at 60° to boundaries; in TM: randomly distributed
truth.structures.sample_800T.overall_structure: Multiphase microstructure consisting of ferrite, bainite/tempered martensite, fresh martensite, and retained austenite.
truth.structures.sample_800T.microstructure_li: In B/TM, carbides present; detailed distribution not specified for this condition
truth.structures.sample_850T.overall_structure: Multiphase microstructure consisting of ferrite, bainite/tempered martensite, fresh martensite, and retained austenite.
truth.structures.sample_850T.microstructure_li: Carbides in B/TM and FM; local oversaturation of alloying elements increases brittleness

prediction.structures.sample_750T.overall_structure: Multiphase heterogeneous microstructure composed of ferrite (F), bainite/tempered martensite (B/TM), fresh martensite (FM), and retained austenite (RA)
prediction.structures.sample_800T.overall_structure: Multiphase heterogeneous microstructure composed of ferrite (F), bainite/tempered martensite (B/TM), fresh martensite (FM), and retained austenite (RA)
prediction.structures.sample_850T.overall_structure: Multiphase heterogeneous microstructure composed of ferrite (F), bainite/tempered martensite (B/TM), fresh martensite (FM), and retained austenite (RA)

truth.interfaces.sample_750T.defect_interactio: Strain gradients at ferrite/hard domain interfaces generate GNDs and back stress, enhancing hardening and delaying microcrack initiation.
truth.interfaces.sample_750T.interface_notes[0: Tensile stress concentrates in ferrite near interfaces, compressive stress in hard domains, promoting strain partitioning and continuity.
truth.interfaces.sample_750T.interface_notes[1: Dislocation slip hindered in ferrite by back stress; hard domains soften to accommodate deformation.
truth.interfaces.sample_750T.phase_evolution: During deformation, RA transforms to DIM (TRIP effect), altering phase fractions and contributing to strain hardening.
truth.interfaces.sample_800T.defect_interactio: Higher GND density compared to 750T leads to stronger back stress hardening (σ_B=300 MPa at necking).
truth.interfaces.sample_800T.interface_notes[0: Strain localization at two points, expanding at 45° and merging, causing necking.
truth.interfaces.sample_800T.interface_notes[1: Coordinated deformation between soft and hard domains, but less pronounced strain partitioning than in 750T due to lower RA content.
truth.interfaces.sample_800T.phase_evolution: Limited TRIP effect; RA content decreases, DIM contributes less to hardening.
truth.interfaces.sample_850T.defect_interactio: Hard domains are cut through (due to brittleness), crack tip blunting less effective; back stress contribution σ_B=278 MPa at necking.
truth.interfaces.sample_850T.interface_notes[0: Severe stress concentration at interfaces; hard phases severed, leading to quasi-cleavage fracture.
truth.interfaces.sample_850T.interface_notes[1: Limited strain partitioning; hard domains fail prematurely.
truth.interfaces.sample_850T.phase_evolution: Minimal RA available for TRIP; negligible DIM formation.

prediction.interfaces.sample_750T.defect_interactio: Dislocations accumulate at ferrite and bainite/martensite boundaries to coordinate phases with different mechanical responses during deformation.
prediction.interfaces.sample_750T.interface_notes[0: Back stress generated in ferrite (compressive) and adjacent B/TM/FM (tensile direction) maintains deformation continuity; strain gradient drives GND accumulation at boundaries.
prediction.interfaces.sample_750T.phase_evolution: During deformation, RA transforms to DIM via TRIP effect; the soft domain hardens and the hard domain softens due to coordinated deformation, preventing premature micro-porosity initiation.
prediction.interfaces.sample_800T.defect_interactio: Highest average KAM (0.69°) among three samples due to higher bainite and martensite content; significant strain concentration at boundaries.
prediction.interfaces.sample_800T.interface_notes[0: Back stress generated in ferrite (compressive) and adjacent B/TM/FM (tensile direction) maintains deformation continuity; strain gradient drives GND accumulation at boundaries.
prediction.interfaces.sample_800T.phase_evolution: During deformation, RA transforms to DIM via TRIP effect; coordinated deformation between soft and hard domains observed, with two local strain concentration points expanding at 45° and connecting to induce necking.
prediction.interfaces.sample_850T.defect_interactio: Dislocations accumulate at ferrite and bainite/martensite boundaries; hard domains observed to be severed due to local alloying element oversaturation.
prediction.interfaces.sample_850T.interface_notes[0: Back stress generated in ferrite (compressive) and adjacent B/TM/FM (tensile direction) maintains deformation continuity; lower RA content results in less pronounced TRIP-induced strain-hardening behavior.
prediction.interfaces.sample_850T.phase_evolution: During deformation, limited RA transforms to DIM due to low initial RA content; hard domains become brittle and can be severed, providing less crack blunting than in AN750-T and AN800-T.

truth.properties.sample_750T.mechanical.tensil: Relatively uniform strain during yield; stress partitioning observed in uniform plastic stage (DIC).
truth.properties.sample_800T.mechanical.tensil: Two local strain concentration points appear, expand at 45° and connect, leading to necking.
truth.properties.sample_850T.mechanical.tensil: Strain concentration in hard domains; necking occurs without significant strain partitioning.

truth.characterization_methods.sample_750T.pur: Local strain distribution during tensile test
truth.characterization_methods.sample_750T.key: Uniform strain at yield; stress partitioning and strain redistribution during plastic deformation.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_800T.key: Two local strain concentration points; expansion and connection at 45° leading to necking.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_850T.key: Strain concentration in hard regions; no significant strain partitioning.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_750T.pur: Monitor local strain distribution during uniaxial tensile deformation
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_750T.key: Relatively uniform strain during yielding; stress redistribution observed during uniform plastic deformation; strain concentration and re-concentration behaviors linked to TRIP effect and multiphase coordination.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_800T.pur: Monitor local strain distribution during uniaxial tensile deformation
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_800T.key: Two local strain concentration points observed during deformation; as strain progresses, these points expand at 45° and connect with each other, leading to necking.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_850T.pur: Monitor local strain distribution during uniaxial tensile deformation
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_850T.key: Strain concentration and re-concentration observed; coordinated strain between soft and hard domains prevents premature failure despite low RA content.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.simulation_m: Iso-work theory + von Mises equivalent back stress + Mecking-Kocks dislocation evolution + Olson-Cohen martensite kinetics + Hall-Petch
truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Model fits well with experimental flow stress; back stress and phase contributions quantified.
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Assumes equal tensile work across phases to distribute flow stress and strain increment.
σ_F dε_F = σ_B/TM dε_B/TM = σ_FM dε_FM = σ_RA dε_RA = σ_DIM dε_DIM
Martensitic transformation kinetics to calculate f_DIM from RA fraction.
Grain boundary strengthening for F and RA.
σ0_F=100 MPa, K_F=600 MPa·μm^{1/2}; σ0_RA=180 MPa, K_RA=149 MPa·μm^{1/2}
Dislocation contribution to flow stress for all phases.
α_BCC=0.38, α_FCC=0.35; M_j from EBSD; G_j, b_j given in Table A2
k1, k2, A as per phase; initial ρ0 listed in Table A2
Calculates back stress from GND gradient.
ν=0.28, G, b, R, Δh (values in Table A2)

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.simulation_m: Constitutive modeling based on iso-work theory with von Mises equivalent back-stress calculation and dislocation density evolution
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Flow stress curves predicted by the model show strong alignment with experimental data for AN750-T, AN800-T, and AN850-T; back stress values at necking strain (217 MPa, 300 MPa, 278 MPa respectively) are consistent with DIC strain fields and TEM GND observations.
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Multiphase flow stress superposition assuming equal work distribution among F, B/TM, FM, RA, and DIM
Eq. (1)-(2); Ph = {F, B/TM, FM, RA, DIM}
Deformation-induced martensite transformation kinetics from retained austenite
Grain boundary strengthening for ferrite and retained austenite
σ_0F = 100 MPa, K_F = 600 MPa·μm^1/2; σ_0RA = 180 MPa, K_RA = 149 MPa·μm^1/2
Dislocation strengthening contribution for each phase
α_BCC = 0.38, α_FCC = 0.35; G_F = 66.0 GPa, G_B/TM/FM/DIM = 81.6 GPa, G_RA = 72.0 GPa; b_BCC = 0.248 nm, b_FCC = 0.250 nm
Evolution of dislocation density with true strain
Back stress from geometrically necessary dislocations at soft-hard domain interfaces
Eq. (19); Δρ_GND and Δh obtained from TEM and EBSD

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: AN750-T achieved a product of strength and elongation (PSE) of 36.3 GPa%.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: AN800-T: YS 985 MPa, UTS 1295 MPa, elongation 18.7%.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: AN850-T: YS 991 MPa, UTS 1269 MPa, elongation 13.9%.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: No dynamic propagation instabilities (Lüders bands or Portevin-Le Chatelier bands) were observed in any sample.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: The yield strength of the developed multiphase steel is 2-3 times higher than traditional low-alloy steel.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Annealing at 750 °C with tempering produced an optimum combination: tensile strength 1068 MPa, fracture elongation 34.0%.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Back stress at necking strain: AN750-T 236 MPa, AN800-T 291 MPa, AN850-T 254 MPa.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Dimple size distribution analyzed by Nano Measure showed a bimodal distribution; AN750-T had larger average dimple sizes, indicating effective stress concentration alleviation.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: KAM_AVE values: AN800-T exhibited the highest average KAM (0.69°), attributed to higher content of bainite and martensite.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Critical phase transformation temperatures measured: Ac1=705 °C, Ac3=950 °C, Bf=380 °C, Ms=346 °C.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_10.value: The size of deformation-induced martensite (c) used in parent grain analysis: AN750-T 0.12 μm, AN800-T 0.15 μm, AN850-T 0.19 μm.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_11.value: Table A1 provides detailed phase compositions (wt%) for each element in F, B/TM, FM, RA, DIM for all three tempered samples.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_12.value: Table A2 lists constitutive model parameters including Taylor factors, shear moduli, Burgers vectors, dislocation evolution parameters, initial dislocation densities, and geometric parameters R and Δh.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_13.value: Fig. A1 details the dilatometry procedure for determining critical temperatures.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_14.value: Fig. A2 illustrates the parent grain analysis strategy for estimating DIM size c.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_15.value: The paper emphasizes that the multi-level microstructure design effectively prevents microcrack generation and mitigates localized stress concentrations via stress/strain partitioning between soft ferrite and hard bainite/martensite domains.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Hot-rolled microstructure prior to cold rolling primarily consists of Bainite (B) with a small amount of Fresh Martensite (FM), entirely BCC structure.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Intercritical annealing at 750, 800, and 850°C produces annealed samples (AN750, AN800, AN850) with decreasing retained austenite content as annealing temperature increases, confirmed by XRD (Fig. 3(e)-(f)); exact volume fractions for annealed-only states not tabulated in text.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Maximum back stress (σ_B) at necking strain reached 217 MPa for AN750-T, 300 MPa for AN800-T, and 278 MPa for AN850-T, contributing significantly to strain hardening beyond iso-work model predictions.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Fracture analysis (Fig. 11): microvoids nucleate mainly at soft-hard domain interfaces; AN750-T and AN800-T show crack tip blunting and secondary cracks at hard domain interfaces, while AN850-T exhibits severed hard domains due to local alloying element oversaturation; dimple size distribution is bimodal with AN750-T having the largest average dimple size.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Post-fracture EBSD of necking region (Fig. 12) confirms RA consumption via TRIP and shows stress concentration in B/TM and FM hard domains; sharp orientation difference and minimum Kikuchi pattern quality occur at phase boundaries.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: EPMA line scans (Fig. 8) show carbon is mainly concentrated in B/TM, FM, and RA; carbon distribution becomes more uniform with increasing annealing temperature, while Mn, Al, and Si distributions show no significant differences. Average elemental compositions of phases are summarized in Table A1.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Fig. 6(c) provides tabulated engineering stress-strain data points for the three tempered samples; AN750-T stress values approximately 0, 900, 1068, 1068, 1068, 900 MPa at engineering strains 0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 34%.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Tensile test conditions: specimens cut along rolling direction (RD) per GB/T 228.1-2021, gauge dimensions 14 mm × 5 mm × 1 mm; tested at room temperature with strain rate 5×10^-4 s^-1 using Instron 3382.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Strength-ductility product (PSE) of AN750-T reaches 36.3 GPa%.
